title: phegopteris straminea
intro: Phegopteris straminea, also known as the yellow sori or stramineous maidenhair, is a species of fern native to East Asia and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Phegopteris straminea include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and improving respiratory function. In herbal preparations, Phegopteris straminea is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and can also be dried and used in capsules or tinctures.
